generated_from: real artifacts plus canonical frontier status docs; sanitized static replay
claim_ceiling: Static replay of real run artifacts, not live product execution.
telemetry_claim_ceiling: OpenTelemetry-shaped metrics are a planned diagnostic expansion; current values are derived, estimated, or explicitly unavailable and cannot by themselves prove product green.

challenge_ladder.label: Easy01
challenge_ladder.challenge: Controlled compiled/no-source route admission, readable artifacts, and basic semantic checkpoint proof.
challenge_ladder.intent: Prove the compiled/no-source route works at all.
challenge_ladder.pipeline_pressure: Admission, Ghidra route entry, artifact readability, and basic semantic checkpoint evidence.
challenge_ladder.trust_gate: Route authority, semantic checkpoint alignment, durable artifacts, and S07 closeout must all be present.
challenge_ladder.why_it_matters: This is the smallest controlled proof that the pipeline can turn a compiled input into reviewable evidence without source-assisted shortcuts.

challenge_ladder.label: Medium01
challenge_ladder.challenge: Shared-code and multi-target attribution without blending evidence across targets.
challenge_ladder.intent: Prove attribution survives more targets and shared-code pressure.
challenge_ladder.pipeline_pressure: Target fanout, shared callable surfaces, per-target evidence separation, and semantic checkpoint preservation.
challenge_ladder.trust_gate: Recovered evidence must stay target-attributed; no blended or borrowed proof across unrelated targets.
challenge_ladder.why_it_matters: This is where the system starts needing evidence lineage, not just a successful single-route run.

challenge_ladder.label: Medium02
challenge_ladder.challenge: Current medium product-route anchor for route authority, comparability, and repeatable semantic proof.
challenge_ladder.intent: Use a stronger repeatable product-route anchor before hard-family pressure.
challenge_ladder.pipeline_pressure: Route authority stability, safe-to-compare gating, repeatable semantic proof, and comparable closeout.
challenge_ladder.trust_gate: safe_to_compare must be true and the semantic proof must remain stable across the replayed target set.
challenge_ladder.why_it_matters: This is the medium anchor for showing the replay is comparable, not just visually green.

challenge_ladder.label: Hard01
challenge_ladder.challenge: Enterprise survivability pressure: branch trust, rejected-object evidence, replay trust, and closeout ceiling.
challenge_ladder.intent: Prove enterprise survivability behavior without weakening claim ceilings.
challenge_ladder.pipeline_pressure: Branch trust, rejected-object evidence, replay trust, object-bound authority, and hard closeout discipline.
challenge_ladder.trust_gate: Object-bound authority must survive closeout; diagnostic branch evidence cannot be silently promoted.
challenge_ladder.why_it_matters: This is the first hard proof that the system can preserve trusted and rejected decisions through closeout.

challenge_ladder.label: Hard02
challenge_ladder.challenge: Stale-attestation survivability: prove stale invalidation, audit forwarding, and object-bound authority without overclaiming product-green.
challenge_ladder.pipeline_pressure: Invalidation, audit forwarding, stale state handling, and standing-baseline promotion discipline.
challenge_ladder.trust_gate: A comparable finish-line signal is not automatically product-green; promotion requires replay-backed closeout authority.
challenge_ladder.current_status: Frontier signal, not bundled as a sanitized public replay.
challenge_ladder.why_it_matters: This shows the next hard-family pressure without pretending the public demo has the full replay artifact root.
challenge_ladder.blocker: not bundled as a sanitized public replay; current truth marks a comparable finish-line signal but not product-green promotion

challenge_ladder.label: Hard03
challenge_ladder.challenge: Unsupported-target quarantine and unsafe-path denial pressure, currently limited by analyzer/runtime and reopen-gate authority before product semantics can be claimed.
challenge_ladder.intent: Stress unsupported-target quarantine and unsafe-path denial.
challenge_ladder.pipeline_pressure: Analyzer/runtime authority, route-event queue repair, quarantine boundaries, and semantic proof admission.
challenge_ladder.trust_gate: Semantic quarantine cannot be claimed until the analyzer/runtime boundary is stable and replay-backed.
challenge_ladder.why_it_matters: This row is intentionally visible because a useful product demo should show where the product is still blocked.
challenge_ladder.blocker: reservation_stage analyze API read timeout before runtime entry; current branch is a bounded reopen gate after route-event-queue repair
challenge_ladder.claim_ceiling: active Hard03 blocker signal, not public replay

runs.label: Hard03
runs.claim_ceiling: active Hard03 blocker signal, not public replay

runs.label: Batch B
runs.claim_ceiling: partial package-scale diagnostic replay; closeout stalled
